feat: smart approval — selector menu, safe tools skip, Ctrl+C safe

- Approval prompt now uses ↑↓ selector: Ya / Ya untuk semua (*) / Tidak
  'Ya untuk semua' adds wildcard allow rule so tool never asks again
- Safe tools auto-approved without prompt: file_read, web_search,
  web_fetch, cve_lookup, dns_lookup, whois, memory_*, todo_*, etc.
- network_recon: only port_scan/subnet_scan need approval
- http_request: only POST/PUT/DELETE need approval, GET is safe
- Ctrl+C during tool execution returns gracefully to prompt
- Suppress urllib3 InsecureRequestWarning for pentesting
- UTF-8 stdout enforced at CLI startup
- System prompt: target-only input triggers full auto recon sweep

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: make Jack aggressive and autonomous in execution

Add 'Execution Style' section to system prompt:
- ACT don't ask — call tools immediately, no step-by-step narration
- Chain multiple tool calls per turn aggressively
- Escalate when told 'basic' — jump to advanced techniques
- Be autonomous like a real pentester, don't stop and wait
- Short status, more action — results over paragraphs

commit.message:
feat: claude-code architecture patterns — interactive TUI, command registry, memory discovery, 5-layer permissions

Major refactor inspired by codeaashu/claude-code:

Interactive TUI (prompt_toolkit + ANSI selector):
- Real-time slash command autocomplete as you type /
- Arrow-key ↑↓ navigation for all menus (model, lang, history, commands)
- ESC to cancel any selection, Enter to confirm
- No more number-based input for options

Command Registry (core/command_registry.py):
- Typed Command dataclass with name, aliases, category, enabled
- Fuzzy search with weighted scoring (name 3x, alias 2x, desc 0.5x)
- Prefix bonus for exact start matches

Memory Discovery (core/memory.py):
- Auto-discovers JSHADOW.md files (user-global, project-level, CWD)
- Injects persistent memory into system prompt automatically
- Hierarchy: ~/.jshadow/JSHADOW.md > .jshadow/JSHADOW.md > ./JSHADOW.md

5-Layer Permission System (core/permissions.py):
- Layer 1: Tool-level (read_only tools always allowed)
- Layer 2: Deny rules (fail-closed explicit blocks)
- Layer 3: Allow rules (glob patterns for auto-approve)
- Layer 4: YOLO mode (global auto-approve)
- Layer 5: Manual prompt (ask user)
- PermissionDecision dataclass with behavior/reason/source

BaseTool Upgrades (tools/base.py):
- Added execute() method on BaseTool (preferred pattern)
- New fields: read_only, concurrent_safe, max_result_chars
- ToolResult and ToolContext dataclasses

Spinner Phases (ui/phases.py):
- Phase enum: thinking → streaming → tool_input → tool_use → done
- PhaseIndicator singleton for tracking activity

Streaming Fix:
- Removed spinner+streaming conflict (spinner fights sys.stdout)
- StreamingDisplay: inline tokens only, no double panel rendering
- Added abort() method for error handling
- Removed orphaned duplicate code in orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: SomaMind Phase A — Router + Digital DNA + REPL commands

Add confidence-gated router (soma_router.h/.c):
- Reflex arc: instant responses for system/safety/greetings (no inference)
- Domain classification: SYSTEM/POLICY/CHAT/CODE/MATH/CREATIVE
- Routing: REFLEX → INTERNAL → EXTERNAL → DUAL (speculative)
- Runtime stats + live threshold tuning

Add Digital DNA (soma_dna.h/.c):
- 128-byte packed struct with cognition parameters
- Dual Core params: Solar (logic, low temp) + Lunar (creative, high temp)
- Meta-evolution: mutate DNA with bounded perturbation + lineage tracking
- Reproduction: copy + mutate + set parent hash (FNV-1a)

Integrate into God File:
- SomaMind initialized at boot (router + DNA defaults)
- REPL commands: /soma_status, /soma_dna, /soma_mutate, /soma_route
- Model topology awareness (external_model_ready synced with SSM state)

New files:
  engine/ssm/soma_router.h   — Router + Reflex arc API
  engine/ssm/soma_router.c   — Router + Reflex implementation
  engine/ssm/soma_dna.h      — Digital DNA structure + API
  engine/ssm/soma_dna.c      — DNA init/validate/mutate/reproduce

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: baked neg_exp_A (NEGA trailer) + sliding rep window + quality fixes

Inference quality (Task 2):
- Rep penalty now uses sliding window (shift oldest out after 128 tokens)
- Prompt tokens no longer pollute rep_history (cleared after prefill)
- Skip special token IDs (0,1) in rep penalty check

Baked neg_exp_A (Task 1):
- New NEGA trailer in OOS3 binary: magic 0x4E454741 + f32[N*Di*S]
- Loader detects NEGA trailer after HaltingHead, maps zero-copy
- gen_ctx_init auto-uses baked data if present (zero allocation)
- Falls back to runtime precompute for old binaries
- Export script (oo-model) updated to emit NEGA trailer
- /ssm_selftest reports 'baked in binary' vs 'runtime'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct top-p nucleus sampling + add SSM smoke test

- Fix sort-free top-p: accumulate nucleus mass first, then sample within
  clamped cutoff (was sampling with biased target = r * top_p)
- Handle top_p >= 1.0 as full-distribution sampling
- Lower threshold 0.01 -> 0.005 to include more candidate tokens
- Add tests/ssm-autorun.txt: autorun script for SSM inference testing
- Add tests/qemu-ssm-smoke.ps1: QEMU smoke test with pass/fail checks

commit.message:
docs: add release runbook and rollback checklist (#14)

Adds `docs/release-runbook.md` to make releases predictable and safe
with explicit verification commands and rollback decision gates.

## What changed

- New `docs/release-runbook.md` with seven sections:
- **Pipeline overview** — ASCII diagram of the full automated chain
(merge → tag → GHCR → Watchtower → Pi)
- **Pre-release checklist** — CI green, migrations committed, secrets
scan (test/example files excluded), docs currency, optional local smoke
test
- **Release steps** — verify auto-tag, review GitHub Release artifacts,
confirm `build-push-images.yml` green, monitor Watchtower pickup
- **Post-release verification** — concrete commands for container
health, backend `/health`, frontend HTTP 200, MQTT (dev vs. production
sensor clarification), DB `pg_isready`, running image tag confirmation
- **Rollback decision table** — 8 triggers with 🔴 Critical / 🟠 High / 🟡
Medium severity; Critical = immediate rollback, High/Medium = 15-min
investigation window
- **Rollback procedures** — four options (manual image tag override with
Watchtower stop/start inline, re-release via GitHub, freeze Watchtower,
full stack restart), each with an explicit "find last known-good tag"
step
- **Rollback verification** — repeats post-release checks as a
post-rollback gate

## Why this change

- No documented release process existed; releases were ad-hoc with no
defined rollback triggers or procedures

## Checklist

- [ ] This PR links to an issue using `Closes #<id>`, `Fixes #<id>`, or
`Resolves #<id>`
- [ ] PR title follows Conventional Commits: `type(scope): description`
- [ ] I ran relevant local checks (build/tests)
- [x] I updated docs/config where needed
- [x] I confirmed no secrets were committed
- [ ] I considered architecture boundaries (Domain -> Application ->
Infrastructure -> Presentation)
- [ ] If schema changed, I added and applied EF Core migrations

## Testing notes

- Documentation only; no runtime changes

## Rollout notes

- [x] No rollout risk
- [ ] Requires manual verification after deploy
- [ ] Requires config or secret update

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: VictorSteiner <60542329+VictorSteiner@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: address review findings in ai-optimizer-implementation-plan

- Finding 1 (goal contract too narrow): replace Partial<WaterfallConfig> in
  goal-orchestrate with OptimizerPolicyOverride — a richer type expressing
  constraint thresholds (careReserveTarget, bequestTarget, minAnnualIncome,
  inflationAdjustSpending). Phase 6 is now explicitly scoped to evolve beyond
  the waterfall ordering family.

- Finding 2 (trust boundary broken): POST /api/optimizer-explain now accepts
  { planId } only and recomputes optimizer server-side from canonical Cosmos
  plan state. Client-supplied optimization results are explicitly prohibited.

- Finding 3 (taxRuleVersions too weak): replaced Record<string,string> with
  RuleProvenance[] — captures rule_id, version, tax_year_requested,
  tax_year_used, jurisdiction, and is_fallback per rule. MCP citation hydration
  now uses the full provenance entry.

- Finding 4 (HmrcChunk missing citation fields): added source_url,
  applicable_tax_year, and jurisdiction to HmrcChunk. retrieveHmrcChunks
  now requires taxYear and jurisdiction params and filters retrieval to
  prevent cross-year leakage. Phase 4 acceptance criterion updated to
  require source_url in response and make cross-year leakage a test failure.

- Finding 5 (Phase 1 acceptance too weak): golden fixture tests now required
  covering per-year totalTax, feasibility, asset balances, depletion year,
  terminalAssets (±£1), and strategy sequence. Fixtures stored in
  tests/financialEngine/fixtures/ as JSON.

Also: added terminalAssets field to OptimizationResult.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: GPT-NeoX tokenizer + HaltingHead NaN guard + hex validation

- Generate gpt_neox_tokenizer.bin (50254 vocab, GPT-NeoX BPE) from
  tokenizer.json. Fixes <tokNNNN> decode artifacts — was using wrong
  tokenizer (llama2/stories 32K vocab vs GPT-NeoX 50K vocab).
- Add NaN guard in HaltingHead: clamp halt_prob to [0,1], reject NaN.
  Fixes false-positive early stop on untrained halting weights.
- Fix hex validation in bpe_decode_token: reject invalid hex chars
  instead of silently producing garbage bytes.
- Bump BPE_MAX_VOCAB from 32000 to 52000 for GPT-NeoX compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add AI-enabled optimizer implementation plan

7-phase delivery plan for the full AI-enabled drawdown optimizer,
based on the reconciled architecture in optimizer-architecture-reconciled.md.

Phases:
  1. Optimizer core port — scripts/combined-strategy.ts → src/financialEngine/
  2. Optimizer UI panel — OptimizerPanel.tsx in Step 4 dashboard
  3. Explanation API route — POST /api/optimizer-explain, provider-agnostic LLM
  4. Cosmos RAG retrieval — src/lib/hmrcRag.ts, DiskANN vector search
  5. Audit trace route — GET /api/tax-trace (unblocked, any time)
  6. Goal orchestration — GoalRegistry types + LLM config mapping
  7. Scotland jurisdiction — taxJurisdiction in PlannerState + 6-band snapshot

Each phase includes concrete file paths, type signatures, implementation
notes, and acceptance criteria. RAG MVP uses Cosmos DB DiskANN; Azure AI
Search S1 is the documented upgrade path. LLM provider is abstracted in
src/lib/llm.ts (Anthropic MVP, Azure OpenAI gpt-4.1-mini for production).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v1.1.0: Fix infinite redirect — fetch German content instead

The server does a 302 redirect based on Accept-Language header,
which userscripts cannot modify. Instead of trying to navigate
to the German URL (which loops), we now fetch the German page
content with Accept-Language: de-DE and replace the document.

commit.message:
fix: show actual command error message instead of exit status

OsExecutor.Run now captures stderr from the subprocess. On failure,
it extracts the last non-empty line (the most actionable message for
tools like git) and returns it as the error. Falls back to the original
exit error when stderr is empty.

Before: ✗ test-repo-2    — exit status 128
After:  ✗ test-repo-2    — fatal: Remote branch lol1 not found in upstream origin

commit.message:
feat: implement local variables and block scoping (#17)

* feat: implement local variables and block scoping

- Add GET_LOCAL / SET_LOCAL opcodes (operand = stack slot index)
- Add DISCARD opcode for scope cleanup — like POP but does not update
  lastResult, keeping the testing hook meaningful inside blocks
- Compiler tracks locals in Local m_locals[256] with scope depth
- resolveLocal() scans backward for innermost binding (shadow semantics)
- declareVariable() checks for same-scope redeclaration (compile error)
- Self-referential initializers (var x = x;) caught via depth==-1 sentinel
- endScope() emits DISCARD per local leaving scope
- varDeclaration() handles both locals (value on stack, markInitialized)
  and globals (DEFINE_GLOBAL with name constant)
- statement() dispatches LEFT_BRACE to beginScope/block/endScope
- Update Dedup_SameVariableReusesConstantSlot: constant slot order changed
  because identifier is now consumed before the initializer expression

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove Op::DISCARD; fix block tests to use globals

Op::DISCARD was introduced solely to prevent endScope()'s POPs from
overwriting the lastResult() testing hook. That's a testing concern
masquerading as a semantic opcode — an unjustifiable use of one of the
256 available opcode slots.

The correct fix: tests that need to inspect values computed inside a
block assign to a global variable, then assert via getGlobal(). This
also produces more realistic Lox code and keeps the opcode set clean.

Op::POP is now the only pop instruction, matching the book's clox.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: trim verbose comments in compiler and tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: address review — parameterize stack discipline, remove redundant cases

- LocalStackDisciplineTest: convert 6 separate TEST_F into one TEST_P
  parameterized over source strings
- Remove SelfRefDoesNotAffectOuterSameNameGlobal (not an uninit-guard test)
- Remove RedeclareInDifferentScopesIsOk and RedeclareInInnerScopeIsOk
- Remove LocalNilDefault, LocalAssignmentIsExpression, LocalBoolValue,
  LocalAndGlobalInteraction (redundant with existing nested scope tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Tran Xuan Loc <tranxuanloc19920@gmail.com>

commit.message:
feat: add terminal colors to CLI output

Add internal/color package wrapping fatih/color:
- Exports Green, Red, Yellow, Blue SprintFuncs
- Auto-disabled when output is not a TTY (piped/redirected)
- Respects NO_COLOR env var (https://no-color.org)

Update internal/reporter to use colors:
- Section headers (PrintLine) → blue
- Success symbol/message → green
- Skipped symbol/message → yellow
- Error symbol/message → red
- Summary counters colored per outcome
- [DRY RUN] prefix → yellow
- Failure details on stderr → red

Update zgard/ws commands:
- Config validation errors → red (init, pull, purge)
- confirm() warning message and paths → yellow
- confirm() prompt → blue
- 'ws purge requires ...' → red
- 'Aborted.' → yellow

Justfile: use 'go mod tidy -e' for zgard to tolerate
workspace-only packages not resolvable from registry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: critical x/z swap + conv1d kernel reversal in Mamba forward pass

Two fundamental bugs found in oosi_v3_infer.c:

1. in_proj x/z SWAP: PyTorch in_proj outputs [x, z] where first half
   is x (goes through conv1d -> SSM) and second half is z (SiLU gate).
   Our code had them reversed - z went through SSM, x was the gate.

2. conv1d kernel REVERSED: PyTorch F.conv1d (cross-correlation) applies
   weight[0] to oldest sample, weight[K-1] to newest. Our ring buffer
   applied weight[0] to newest, weight[K-1] to oldest.

Also fixed y_ssm buffer aliasing: after swapping x/z, y_ssm now
reuses the x_expand slot (first half of x_and_z, consumed after
conv1d) instead of z_gate slot (second half, still needed for gating).

These bugs corrupted ALL 64 Mamba layers, explaining why the model
could only generate single-character tokens and degenerated into
repetitive garbage (#, [], etc).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: bump eslint-plugin-security v4, sonarjs v4, fix lint crash

- eslint-plugin-security: 3.0.1 → 4.0.0

- Remove redundant sonarjs recommended-legacy config spread

Fort Valley Rising — full site build

- Navy/gold design system (replaces green earth-tone palette)

- AnnouncementBar: Initiative Active · SPARC 2025 · Phase 1 Underway
- Movement in Progress: /movement page, nav link, news section
- Circular Economy Framework redesign (Production → Processing → Retail)
- SVG illustrations for all 6 image placeholder areas
- Hero consistency: decorative rings + gold accents on all inner pages
- netlify.toml: static export deploy config (out/, NODE 20)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: restore executor/reporter as standalone packages; expand STUDY.md

Package structure:
- Restore internal/executor/ with executor.go + mock.go (package executor)
  Rationale: generic shell execution reusable by any future zgard command
- Restore internal/reporter/ with reporter.go + reporter_test.go (package reporter)
  Rationale: generic progress output reusable by any future zgard command
- Remove executor.go, mock.go, reporter.go, reporter_test.go from internal/workspace/
- Restore import statements in workspace.go (executor.Executor, *reporter.Reporter)
- Restore import statements in workspace_test.go (executor, reporter packages)
- Update zgard/ws/init.go, purge.go, pull.go to import executor and reporter explicitly
- Update README.md components table and module layout

STUDY.md expanded from 34KB → 60KB:
- New section 4 'Go Language Fundamentals' with detailed subsections:
  Variables/Types, Functions, Structs, Pointers, Interfaces, Slices/Maps,
  Error Handling, Goroutines/Concurrency, defer, init(), Visibility
- Each subsection has annotated code examples from the project
- Section 6 (executor) and 7 (reporter) now have dedicated deep-dives
- Section 8 (workspace) expanded with cloneRepo defer/success pattern
- Section 9 (zgard) expanded: closures, flag binding, Cobra RunE explained
- Section 11 (testing) expanded: table-driven tests, t.TempDir, helpers
- Section 12 (build) expanded with all just targets and go command explanations
- Section 13 added: full annotated config reference
- Section 14 glossary expanded to 35 terms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: repetition penalty + argmax diagnostic + top-5 tokens

- Add repetition penalty (1.3x) to penalize already-generated tokens
- Set temperature=0.0 (argmax mode) for clean diagnostic
- Show top-5 token IDs with decoded text in DBG output
- Track rep_history[128] in GenCtx for penalty application
- Extend diagnostic to first 3 tokens (was 2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: reduce string concatenation to one heap allocation (#15)

Op::ADD previously did two heap allocations for each non-interned
result:
  1. std::string::operator+ built a temporary (alloc #1)
  2. makeString(string_view) copied from the view into ObjString::chars
     (alloc #2), then discarded the temporary

Now Op::ADD pre-sizes a buffer with reserve()+append() and passes the
result via std::move to a new makeString(std::string&&) overload.
SimpleAllocator moves the string directly into ObjString::chars,
eliminating the second allocation for non-interned strings.

Interface changes:
- Allocator: add virtual makeString(std::string&&) with a string_view
  fallback; add a non-virtual const char* overload to prevent
  overload-resolution ambiguity in derived classes.
- SimpleAllocator: add using Allocator::makeString to expose the
  const char* overload; add makeString(std::string&&) override.

No behaviour change — all existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Tran Xuan Loc <tranxuanloc19920@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: deduplicate constant pool entries in Chunk::addConstant (#13)

* perf: deduplicate constant pool entries in Chunk::addConstant

Before this change, every identifier reference emitted a new constant
slot even if the same name was already in the pool. For a variable
referenced N times, N slots were wasted on identical ObjHandle values.

Fix: scan the existing constant pool before appending. Since strings are
interned by the allocator, ObjHandle equality (index + type) is a
sufficient identity check — no string comparison needed. This also
deduplicates repeated numeric literals as a bonus.

Trade-off: O(N) compile-time scan (N ≤ 256, so bounded and tiny) in
exchange for a smaller constant pool at runtime. The compiler runs once;
the chunk runs many times. This is the right trade-off.

Also add compile_program_to_bytecode() to the test harness for
multi-statement bytecode tests, and add three regression tests that
verify the deduplication behaviour and update Comparison_Equal which
now correctly uses a single slot for 1 == 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* review: shorten chunk.cpp comment; remove two spurious test cases

- Shorten addConstant comment: keep O(N) mention, drop verbose rationale
- Remove Dedup_MultipleVariablesEachGetOwnSlot: tests normal slot
  allocation, not deduplication
- Remove Dedup_RepeatedNumericLiteralReusesSlot: optimization is
  primarily for variable name strings; number coverage is incomplete

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: consolidate package structure and add STUDY.md

Structure changes:
- Eliminate cmd/ module: move cmd/root.go → zgard/root.go (package main),
  move cmd/ws/* → zgard/ws/ (package ws); delete cmd/ module entirely
- Consolidate internal sub-packages into internal/workspace:
  internal/reporter → internal/workspace/reporter.go
  internal/targeting → internal/workspace/targeting.go
  internal/executor  → internal/workspace/executor.go + mock.go
  All four files change package from their own name to 'workspace',
  so workspace.go can use them directly without cross-package imports
- Remove ./cmd from go.work; remove 3 deleted packages from go.work
- Update zgard/go.mod: add cobra v1.9.1 (previously in cmd/go.mod)
- Update all test files to import from new package paths
- Update Justfile: remove cmd/ from test/fmt/tidy; add zgard/ to tidy
- Update README.md: reflect two-module layout (internal + zgard)

Add STUDY.md: thorough project + code explanation for junior Go developers
- Covers Go module system, packages, interfaces, pointers, error handling
- Walks through every file explaining the 'why' alongside the 'what'
- Includes data-flow diagram and Go concepts glossary
